definition: Earnings quality is deteriorating through the classic late-cycle pattern: S&P 500 accruals/total assets risen from ~2% (2020-21) to ~4-5%, GAAP-to-non-GAAP gap widened to 10-12% (vs 5-7% historical average), SBC at ~$180-200B annually. Magnitude of overstatement is probably 10-20%, not the initially claimed 25-40%.
evidence: GAAP-to-non-GAAP gap at 10-12% vs 5-7% historical average
open_questions: Whether geopolitical cover for earnings management (cookie-jar reserves) is distorting quality metrics
Whether SBC should be treated as full cash-equivalent cost
What portion of non-GAAP adjustments are legitimately non-recurring
